title: Smart Parking System
subtitle: Automated slot detection with IR sensors + servo barrier
desc: Detect car presence with IR sensors — control entry with a servo!
fullDesc: Build a smart parking system that uses 6 IR sensors to detect occupied and free slots, controls entry/exit with a servo motor barrier, and displays real-time slot availability on a display.
author: Shruti Doke
innovation: Low-cost parking management prototype using IR-based slot detection and automated barrier control, suitable for school labs and demo environments.
problemStatement: Manual parking management is inefficient and prone to errors. There is no easy way to know slot availability without physically checking each space.
solutionApproach: Used 6 IR sensors to detect car presence in each slot, a servo motor as an entry/exit barrier, and a display to show live slot availability — all controlled by an Arduino UNO.

hardwareSpecs.communicationProtocol: Digital I/O (IR sensors), PWM (Servo), I2C or 4-bit parallel (Display)

guide: Connect each IR sensor OUT pin to Arduino digital pins D2–D7; VCC to 5V, GND to GND
Connect servo signal wire to Arduino D9 (PWM pin); VCC to 5V, GND to GND
Wire LCD in 4-bit mode: RS→D10, EN→D11, D4-D7→D12-D13 (adjust as needed)
Connect potentiometer wiper to LCD V0 pin for contrast control
Install the Servo library (built-in) and LiquidCrystal library via Arduino IDE Library Manager
Upload the starter sketch and open Serial Monitor at 9600 baud
Place 3D printed cars in front of IR sensors to simulate parked vehicles
Verify slot detection in Serial Monitor; observe servo opening/closing on entry trigger
Confirm display shows correct free/occupied slot count
prerequisites: Understanding of digital I/O and PWM

codeFiles.name: smart_parking_system.ino
codeFiles.desc: IR sensor slot detection + servo barrier + LCD display
